title: Create npx gru-ai Interactive CLI
description: Create CLI entrypoint for npx gru-ai. Add bin field to package.json. Interactive setup via readline: project name, team size, agent name customization, platform selection. Scaffolds .gruai/ canonical home, symlinks platform dirs, creates .context/ tree, agent personalities, registry, CLAUDE.md. Includes 'gruai start' subcommand to launch dashboard server.

dod.criterion: npx gru-ai shows help with init, start, update subcommands
npx gru-ai init walks through interactive setup (project name, team preset, agent names, platform) using only Node readline
Init scaffolds .gruai/, .context/ tree, agent files, registry, CLAUDE.md, and gruai.config.json
npx gru-ai start launches the dashboard server
npm run build && npm pack produces a working tarball with dist-cli/

tasks.title: Create CLI entrypoint with bin field and subcommand routing
tasks.user_scenario: User runs npx gru-ai and sees a help message with available subcommands (init, start, update); running with no args shows help.
tasks.scope: Create cli/index.ts as the CLI entrypoint with a shebang line. Add bin field to package.json pointing to dist-cli/index.js. Add build:cli script and update files array. Implement subcommand routing: parse process.argv for init, start, update, or show help.

dod.criterion: cli/index.ts exists with #!/usr/bin/env node shebang and subcommand router (init, start, update, help)
package.json has bin field mapping 'gru-ai' to 'dist-cli/index.js'
package.json build script includes cli build, files array includes dist-cli/
npm run build:cli compiles cli/index.ts without TypeScript errors
Running node dist-cli/index.js with no args prints help text listing init, start, and update subcommands

tasks.title: Implement interactive init setup with readline prompts
tasks.user_scenario: User runs npx gru-ai init and is walked through an interactive setup: project name, team size preset, optional agent name customization, and platform selection.
tasks.scope: Create cli/commands/init.ts with the full interactive init flow using Node readline (no external deps). Prompts: project name, team size preset, agent name customization, platform selection. Store collected config in a typed InitConfig object.

dod.criterion: cli/commands/init.ts exists using only Node readline — no external prompt libraries added to dependencies
All four prompt stages work: project name, team preset, agent name customization, platform selection
Custom team preset enforces minimum of COO + CTO + 1 builder role; shows error and re-prompts if not met
InitConfig type is exported with fields: projectName, preset, agents (array with id/name/role), platform
Subcommand router in cli/index.ts calls init handler when argv includes 'init'

tasks.title: Scaffold .gruai/ home, .context/ tree, agents, registry, and CLAUDE.md
tasks.user_scenario: After completing the init prompts, the user's project is fully scaffolded with .gruai/ directory, .context/ tree, agent personality files, agent-registry.json, CLAUDE.md, and gruai.config.json — ready to run /directive.
tasks.scope: Create cli/commands/scaffold.ts that takes InitConfig and scaffolds everything: .gruai/ canonical home, platform symlinks, .context/ tree, agent personality files, agent-registry.json, CLAUDE.md, gruai.config.json, welcome directive.

dod.criterion: Running full init flow creates .gruai/ directory and correct platform symlink
All templates from cli/templates/ are used — no hardcoded template content in scaffold code
.context/ tree is created with vision.md, lessons/index.md, preferences.md, backlog.json, directives/.gitkeep, reports/.gitkeep
Agent files are generated only for roles matching the selected preset, using cli/templates/agent-roles/ templates
agent-registry.json contains only agents/teams for the selected preset

tasks.title: Implement 'gruai start' subcommand to launch dashboard server
tasks.user_scenario: User runs npx gru-ai start and the dashboard server launches, serving the web UI at localhost with live agent status.
tasks.scope: Create cli/commands/start.ts. Check for initialization, then launch the dashboard server. Support --port flag. Handle SIGINT/SIGTERM for clean shutdown.

dod.criterion: cli/commands/start.ts exists and is wired into the subcommand router
Running 'gruai start' in an initialized project spawns the dashboard server process
Running 'gruai start' in an uninitialized project prints an error directing user to run 'gru-ai init' first
--port flag is accepted and passed through to the server process

tasks.title: End-to-end integration, error handling, and build verification
tasks.user_scenario: User can install gru-ai globally or via npx, run the full init flow, and start the dashboard — with clear error messages for any failure mode.
tasks.scope: Wire all pieces together and harden. Verify npx flow, add error handling, add gruai update subcommand, add colored terminal output, ensure build and npm pack work correctly.

dod.criterion: npm run build succeeds including CLI build, and npm pack produces a tarball containing dist-cli/index.js with correct shebang
Re-running 'gru-ai init' in an already-initialized project warns and asks before overwriting
'gru-ai update' backs up existing skills and re-renders CLAUDE.md preserving agent names
All CLI output uses colored ANSI formatting for errors (red), success (green), and info (blue/cyan)
TypeScript strict mode passes for all cli/ files — no type errors
